title: Neobesseya nuttallii
taxonomy: Neobesseya nuttallii is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It is native to Mexico and is a type of woodland or shrubland plant. The plant is classified as a perennial herb, with leaves that are often opposite in arrangement. Neobesseya nuttallii is also known for its blue-purple flowers, which are a distinctive feature of the species. The plant's classification is further supported by its botanical name, which is used to identify and distinguish it from other related species within the Gesneriaceae family.

intro: Neobesseya nuttallii, commonly known as Nuttall's besseya, is a perennial wildflower native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against cell damage.
